page: Google Cloud Platform c4n-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the c4n server family. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 architecture and is configured with 31.0 GB of system memory. This allocation provides approximately 7.75 GB of RAM per virtual core, positioning the instance for workloads requiring a higher memory-to-CPU ratio than standard general-purpose configurations.

The hardware profile includes dedicated CPU allocation, ensuring consistent compute performance without resource contention from other tenants. However, the instance does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage, and accelerated computing tasks are not supported. Additionally, the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

This resource density makes the instance suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel processing power, such as small-to-medium databases, caching layers, and lightweight enterprise applications. The qualitative cost efficiency of this instance lies in its high memory-to-core ratio, allowing users to run memory-bound applications without paying for unnecessary additional compute cores.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4n-highmem-4 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine within the c4n server family, configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 31.0 GB of RAM. This configuration yields a high memory-to-core ratio of 7.75 GB per vCPU, making it suitable for memory-constrained workloads that do not require extensive compute scaling. The instance features dedicated CPU allocation to prevent resource contention, though it lacks local storage and integrated GPU accelerators. Consequently, all storage operations rely on network-attached volumes. This instance is designed for workloads such as small database deployments, caching systems, and memory-intensive application servers where resource density and memory capacity are prioritized over raw CPU core counts or local disk performance.
